item.title: 巴西贷款机构BRB将向信贷担保基金申请6.37亿美元贷款
item.description: 据知情人士透露，巴西贷款机构BRB计划在本月底前向巴西信贷担保基金（FGC）申请33亿雷亚尔（约合6.3737亿美元）贷款，以支持其资本化计划。【原文：Brazilian lender BRB plans to request a 3.3 billion reais ($637.37 million) loan from Brazil's credit guarantee fund, FGC, by the end ​of the month to support its capitalization efforts, according to a ‌person familiar with the matter.】

item.title: Rhoda AI raises $450 million at $1.7 billion valuation, unveils robot intelligence platform
item.description: 罗达人工智能公司周二宣布完成4.5亿美元A轮融资，公司估值达17亿美元，并发布了一套据称能应对工业环境不可预测性的机器人智能系统。【原文：Rhoda AI on Tuesday said it has raised $450 million in ‌a Series A funding round that values the company at $1.7 billion and unveiled a robot intelligence system it says can handle the unpredictability of industrial environments.】

item.title: 随着战争持续，汽油价格上涨和股市波动可能同时冲击美国低收入和高收入消费者。
item.description: 汽油价格上涨和股市动荡正加剧美以对伊战争的风险，可能对美国各阶层消费者造成重创，从而削弱经济增长的关键支撑。此前市场预期，在加强所得税退税、失业率相对较低以及资产价值上升的推动下，今年美国经济将强劲复苏。【原文：Rising gasoline prices and a wobbly stock market are increasing the risk that the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran could hit hard among consumers across the economic spectrum in the United States, undercutting a key prop of economic growth that had been expected to surge this year on the basis of beefed-up income tax refunds, ​relatively low unemployment, and rising asset values.】

item.title: 波音公司表示，线路缺陷可能导致737 MAX客机第一季度交付延迟
item.description: 波音公司周二表示，其窄体737 MAX客机第一季度交付可能因布线缺陷而延迟。这成为这家陷入困境的飞机制造商最新遭遇的波折，正值首席执行官凯利·奥特伯格致力于改善公司声誉之际。【原文：Boeing said on ​Tuesday first-quarter deliveries of its ‌narrow-body 737 MAX jets could face delays due to wiring flaws, the latest ​hiccup for the troubled planemaker ​as CEO Kelly Ortberg looks to ⁠improve its reputation.】

item.title: 索尼因PlayStation商店定价问题面临27亿美元英国诉讼
item.description: 索尼正面临一起价值近20亿英镑（约合27亿美元）的伦敦诉讼，该诉讼指控这家PlayStation制造商利用"垄断地位"抬高数字游戏价格，这是英国近期开庭审理的最新一起大规模消费者诉讼案。【原文：Sony ​is fighting a London lawsuit worth almost 2 billion pounds ($2.7 billion) that alleges ‌the PlayStation maker's "monopoly position" inflated prices for digital games, in the latest mass consumer case to go to trial in Britain.】

item.title: 巴西Frigol公司通过收购屠宰场，目标实现牛肉产量60%的增长
item.description: 巴西牛肉加工企业Frigol首席执行官卢西亚诺·帕斯康向路透社透露，该公司计划通过与罗赖马州两家屠宰场达成合作，在2026年前将产量提升60%，此举还将扩大其在中国和美国市场的准入渠道。【原文：Frigol expects to raise output by 60% in 2026 through deals with two slaughterhouses in Brazil's northern state of Rondonia, which will also expand its access to China and ​the U.S., the beef processor's CEO Luciano Pascon told Reuters.】

item.title: 埃隆·马斯克表示，X Money将于下月进入早期公开访问阶段
item.description: 埃隆·马斯克周二表示，社交媒体平台X的数字支付系统X Money将于下月进入早期公开测试阶段。这位亿万富翁正致力于将X打造成一款"万能应用"。【原文：Elon Musk on Tuesday said X Money, the social ​media platform's digital payment system, ‌will enter early public access next month, as the billionaire pushes ​to transform X into ​an "everything app."】

item.title: 波音公司2月飞机交付量增加，尽管787客机座椅问题仍在困扰
item.description: 波音公司周一表示，2月份交付了51架喷气式飞机，高于1月份的46架，创下这家美国飞机制造商自2018年以来2月份的最高交付量，尽管787机型的商务舱座椅仍面临持续挑战。【原文：Boeing said on Monday it delivered 51 jets in February, an increase from 46 in January and the U.S. planemaker's highest total for February ​since 2018, despite ongoing challenges with premium class seats for 787s.】

item.title: 保时捷计划通过削减成本和燃油发动机重振旗鼓
item.description: 保时捷新任首席执行官很可能在周三的首次财报发布会上加倍推进成本削减，并更大力度押注燃油车，以说服投资者相信他能重振这家陷入困境的跑车制造商。【原文：Porsche's new CEO is likely to double down on ​cost cuts and lean harder into combustion-engine cars as he seeks to persuade investors he can ‌revive the ailing sportscar maker in his first earnings update on Wednesday.】

item.title: 摩根大通聘请资深银行家以强化中型企业投资银行业务
item.description: 据路透社查阅的一份内部备忘录显示，摩根大通聘请菲尔道斯·波霍瓦拉担任董事总经理，以加强该公司在北美地区的中型企业并购团队。【原文：JPMorgan Chase hired Firdaus Pohowalla as a ​managing director to strengthen the ‌firm's North America mid‑cap mergers and acquisitions team, according to an internal ​memo reviewed by Reuters.】

item.title: Meta收购人工智能代理社交网络Moltbook
item.description: Facebook母公司Meta Platforms周二宣布收购人工智能代理社交平台Moltbook，并将该平台创始人纳入其人工智能研究部门。【原文：Facebook parent Meta Platforms said on Tuesday it had acquired Moltbook, a social networking platform built for artificial intelligence agents, bringing the company's ​founders into its AI research division.】

item.title: 德意志银行上调欧美科技板块评级，将软件板块调至"增持"
item.description: 德意志银行周二将美国和欧洲科技板块评级从"减持"上调至"中性"，并将软件板块评级转为"增持"，称因担忧人工智能引发颠覆而持续数月的抛售行情可能已见顶。【原文：Deutsche Bank upgraded the U.S. and European technology sector ​to "neutral" from "underweight" on Tuesday and ‌turned "overweight" on software, saying the months-long rout sparked by fears of ​AI-driven disruption has likely ​run its course.】

item.title: 美国企业内部人士抛售行为在2月激增，市场波动性加剧
item.description: 美国企业内部人士2月份抛售自家公司股票的数量远超买入量，创下自2024年7月以来的最大差距，凸显出在市场动荡的月份里，高管们对购股持谨慎态度。【原文：U.S. corporate insiders sold far more shares in their companies than they bought in February, marking the largest gap since ​July 2024 and underscoring executives' reluctance to purchase stock ‌during a volatile month for markets.】

item.title: 花旗集团上调人工智能资本支出与营收预期，因企业应用加速普及
item.description: 花旗集团周二上调了2026-2030年全球人工智能资本支出与营收预测，理由是企业需求和采用速度加快。【原文：Citigroup on Tuesday raised its global artificial intelligence capital expenditure and revenue forecasts for ​2026-2030, citing faster enterprise demand and ‌adoption.】

item.title: 阿根廷总统米莱向华尔街推销政策之际，中东局势令投资者心惊胆战
item.description: 阿根廷总统哈维尔·米莱周二试图说服投资者，即使伊朗战争推高油价、走强美元并动摇新兴市场，该国经济复苏仍能保持正轨。【原文：President Javier Milei aims on Tuesday to persuade investors that Argentina's economic turnaround can stay on track even as war ​in Iran pushes oil prices higher, strengthens the dollar and rattles emerging markets.】

item.title: 国际金融协会称，2月份流向新兴市场的投资组合资金放缓至220亿美元
item.description: 全球银行业贸易组织周二公布的数据显示，尽管债券和股票市场资金流动仍保持正增长，但2月份外国投资者对新兴市场资产的购买量骤降至220亿美元以下。【原文：Foreign investors sharply slowed their purchases of emerging market assets in February to ​below $22 billion even as flows remained positive across both debt and equities, data from a global banking trade ‌group showed on Tuesday.】

item.title: US existing home sales unexpectedly increase in February
item.description: 美国2月成屋销售意外增长，因抵押贷款利率走低及房价涨幅放缓吸引买家重返市场，但供应持续紧张可能在春季销售旺季抑制交易活动。【原文：U.S. existing home sales unexpectedly increased in February ​as lower mortgage rates and a moderation in house-price growth pulled buyers back into the market, but ‌still-tight supply could constrain activity during the spring selling season.】

item.title: 独家：埃克森美孚首席执行官称中东地区非必要员工已撤离
item.description: 埃克森美孚公司首席执行官达伦·伍兹周二在接受采访时表示，随着美国和以色列对伊朗的战争持续，该公司已从中东业务中撤离非必要员工。【原文：Exxon Mobil has evacuated ​non-essential employees from its operations in the ‌Middle East, CEO Darren Woods said in an interview on Tuesday, as the U.S.-Israel war ​on Iran continues.】

item.title: 精神航空召回停飞飞行员，为摆脱破产保护做准备
item.description: 精神航空公司周二表示，已召回去年停飞的近500名飞行员，这家廉价航空公司正准备结束其第二次破产程序。【原文：Spirit Airlines said on Tuesday it has called back nearly 500 pilots it furloughed last year, as the ​budget carrier prepares to emerge from its second ‌bankruptcy.】

item.title: 美国银行有望在资本监管方面取得胜利，因监管机构正酝酿新的巴塞尔协议草案。
item.description: 美国总统唐纳德·特朗普领导的银行监管机构将在未来几周公布备受期待的规则草案，该草案最终可能降低贷款机构为吸收损失而必须预留的现金储备额度，这可能成为银行业的一项重大胜利。【原文：U.S. President Donald Trump's bank regulators will unveil in coming weeks long-awaited draft rules that could ultimately shrink ​the amount of cash lenders must set aside to absorb losses, in a major potential victory for the industry.】

item.title: 荷兰法院维持原判，强制Meta提供时间线信息流
item.description: 荷兰上诉法院周二维持了去年10月的裁决，要求Meta Platforms允许荷兰地区的Facebook和Instagram用户按时间顺序查看帖子，而非通过基于用户画像的推送方式。【原文：A Dutch appeals court on Tuesday upheld an October ruling requiring ​Meta Platforms to let Facebook and ‌Instagram users in the Netherlands view posts chronologically, rather than via profiling-based feeds.】

item.title: 彭博社报道，亚马逊计划发行370亿至420亿美元债券
item.description: 据彭博社周二报道，知情人士透露，亚马逊公司计划通过最新债券发行筹集约370亿至420亿美元资金。【原文：Amazon.com is targeting about $37 billion to $42 billion in its latest bond sale, Bloomberg News reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the ​matter.】

item.title: 美国就Zoox公司申请部署无方向盘自动驾驶出租车征求公众意见
item.description: 美国国家公路交通安全管理局周二表示，正就亚马逊旗下自动驾驶公司Zoox部署多达2500辆无方向盘专用无人驾驶出租车的计划征求公众意见。【原文：The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said ​Tuesday it is seeking ‌public comments on Amazon.com's self-driving unit Zoox to deploy ​up to 2,500 purpose-built, ​steering-wheel-free robotaxis.】

item.title: 花旗集团预计第一季度投资银行手续费将实现15%左右的增长
item.description: 花旗集团首席执行官简·弗雷泽周二表示，预计该行第一季度投资银行手续费将实现中个位数百分比增长。【原文：Citigroup expects ​mid-teens percentage ‌growth in its ​investment ​banking fees in ⁠the ​first ​quarter, CEO Jane Fraser ​said ​on Tuesday.】

item.title: 人工智能初创公司Thinking Machines成功获得英伟达的资金支持及重大芯片供应协议
item.description: 人工智能初创公司Thinking Machines Lab周二宣布，已与英伟达达成多年期合作协议，将获得重大投资并采购至少1吉瓦的英伟达新一代处理器。【原文：AI startup Thinking Machines Lab said on Tuesday it has struck a multi-year partnership with Nvidia that will see it receive a ​significant investment and procure at least one gigawatt of the chipmaker's ‌next-generation processors.】

item.title: 高通与Wayve合作加速人工智能自动驾驶系统部署
item.description: 高通与英国自动驾驶初创企业Wayve周二宣布，双方正合作开发集成式人工智能系统，旨在帮助汽车制造商快速部署高级驾驶辅助及自动驾驶功能。【原文：Qualcomm and British self-driving startup Wayve said on Tuesday they ‌are collaborating on an integrated artificial intelligence system to help automakers rapidly deploy advanced driver-assistance and automated driving features.】

item.title: 欧盟主席称削减核能是"战略错误"
item.description: 欧盟委员会主席乌尔苏拉·冯德莱恩周二表示，缩减欧洲核能产业是"战略性错误"。当前各国政府正因伊朗战争引发的能源危机而举步维艰。【原文：Reducing Europe's nuclear ‌energy sector was a "strategic mistake", EU chief Ursula Von der Leyen said on Tuesday, as governments grappled with an energy crunch from the Iran war.】

item.title: 法律人工智能初创公司Legora完成5.5亿美元融资，加速美国市场扩张
item.description: 瑞典法律人工智能初创公司Legora周二宣布，已在D轮融资中以55.5亿美元估值筹集5.5亿美元资金，用于加速其在美国市场的扩张。【原文：Sweden-based legal AI startup Legora said on Tuesday it had raised $550 million at ​a $5.55 billion valuation in a Series D ‌funding round to accelerate its expansion across the United States.】

item.title: 科尔士百货预测年度销售额跌幅将超预期
item.description: 科尔士百货周二预测全年销售额将出现超预期下滑，因精打细算的消费者在其百货商店减少了对服装和家居用品等高利润商品的消费，导致其股价盘前下跌约8%。【原文：Kohl's on Tuesday ‌forecast a steeper annual sales drop than expected, as budget-conscious shoppers ​pull back spending ​on higher-margin items including ⁠apparel and homegoods at ​its department stores, sending its ​shares down about 8% premarket.】

item.title: 酱料香料制造商引发收购热潮，GLP-1药物推动美国人追捧辛辣食品
item.description: 美国对减肥的痴迷正为调味品和香料制造商创造一个火热的市场，随着GLP-1药物使用量的激增，消费者渴望在食物中获得更多风味。【原文：America's obsession with weight loss is creating a hot market for ​makers of condiments and spices, as the surge in GLP-1 drug use has consumers craving more flavor ‌in their food.】
